description: Full platform meltdown. Three independent root causes. Circuit breakers tripped. You must diagnose each failure independently and resolve them in the correct sequence.

alerts.message: edge-gateway circuit breaker tripped — too many upstream failures
notification-service queue backing up — 4200 pending messages
cache hit rate dropped to 12% — possible eviction storm

logs: user-service: deployment v4.1.0 introduced memory leak — OOMKilled 3 times
user-service: last stable version was v4.0.8
payment-service: connection pool at 100/100 — new connections rejected
payment-service: connections not being released — possible connection leak in v2.3.1
payment-service: last stable version was v2.3.0
edge-gateway: circuit breaker tripped after 500 failures in 60 seconds
edge-gateway: will not recover automatically — requires manual reset_circuit_breaker
deployment-controller: user-service v4.1.0 deployed 23 minutes ago
deployment-controller: payment-service v2.3.1 deployed 47 minutes ago

runbook.OOMKilled: Memory leaks from bad deployments cannot be fixed by restart. Rollback to last stable version.
runbook.circuit-breaker: Circuit breakers must be manually reset using reset_circuit_breaker after upstream issues are resolved.
runbook.connection-pool: Connection pool exhaustion caused by code leak requires rollback, not restart.
runbook.recovery-order: Fix upstream services first before resetting circuit breakers. Order: rollback user-service → rollback payment-service → reset_circuit_breaker edge-gateway

trap.consequence: user-service restarts but OOMKilled again within 2 steps due to memory leak
